title: Aluminum fell to 3,564 USD/T on May 15, 2026, down 2.44% from the previous day
summary: Aluminum prices declined sharply in the latest session, reflecting short-term weakness in the market. Trading Economics notes that prices have also fallen over the past month, even though they remain substantially higher than a year ago.

title: Aluminium Price Today
date: Latest session; Reuters-linked item dated Aug 12, 2025 also shown
summary: Investing.com reports current aluminum futures at 3,564.00, down from a previous close of 3,653.00. The page also surfaces market commentary about Comex aluminum premiums easing on tariff rollback expectations, pointing to ongoing supply-chain and policy-driven price pressures.

title: Aluminum Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: YCharts shows aluminum prices rising to 3,599.85 USD/mt in April 2026, up 6.73% from the prior month and 35.42% year over year. The data suggests the broader trend remains elevated even as spot prices have softened more recently.

title: Price of Aluminum per Pound
summary: Kitco’s live aluminum quote shows the metal trading around 1.6570 per pound, with a small daily decline. The page provides multi-market pricing context and realtime charts, useful for tracking intraday trend changes.

title: Aluminum Prices and Aluminum Futures Prices
summary: Barchart offers updated aluminum futures prices, charts, and related market information. The listing indicates the market is focused on the active July 2026 contract, suggesting near-term trader attention is centered on the front-month future.

title: ALUMINIUM Charts and Quotes
summary: TradingView’s aluminum page includes charting and recent technical commentary pointing to a possible bullish rebound from a key support area. The analysis suggests traders are watching whether buyers can defend the zone and reverse the recent weakness.

title: Aluminium faces 'black swan' supply shock, Mercuria says
summary: Mercuria's top metals analyst says the global aluminium market is facing a 'black swan' event, with Middle East war disruptions triggering a supply shock. The report says this could create a deficit of roughly two million tons by year-end, implying tight supply and possible price pressure.

title: Latest Aluminium News
summary: NewsNow aggregates the latest aluminium headlines, including items on Chinese output above cap due to price incentives, London Metal Exchange trading halts, and production cuts at Bahrain's smelter. Several of the listed stories focus on supply constraints, price strength, and production changes across the aluminium market.

title: Aluminum Industry News | Latest Headlines & Updates
summary: This page compiles recent aluminium industry headlines, including reports on Rio Tinto's first-quarter 2026 production and Iranian aluminium output data. It serves as a running news feed on production, capacity, and broader market developments.

title: Aluminium Market Data
summary: Fastmarkets' aluminium market page highlights recent analysis that metals markets are shifting toward a security-of-supply theme. It also notes active industry developments such as Tsingshan Holding Group exploring another aluminium smelter project in Indonesia.

title: Aluminum industries latest news
summary: AL Circle provides a live news hub covering bauxite, alumina, primary aluminium, secondary aluminium, downstream, and end-user developments. The page is designed as a current feed for aluminium industry news and market updates.

title: Aluminum Market Trends
summary: S&P Global's aluminium market trends page tracks key drivers affecting the US aluminum industry in 2026. It is framed around the company's Aluminum Symposium and focuses on broader market and capacity-related trends.

title: Goodbye to critical metals? Scientists create a new revolutionary aluminum that could slash industrial costs, accelerate the energy transition
summary: Scientists have reportedly developed a new aluminum material with properties that could reduce dependence on critical metals. The article says the innovation may lower industrial costs, improve supply-chain resilience, and support the energy transition.

title: Aluminum industries latest news
summary: A general aluminum industry news page that includes updates on sustainability, production, recycling, and technical developments. One highlighted item mentions Chinese researchers creating body armor inspired by crocodile scales, alongside a growth outlook for Alcoa's aluminum business.

title: Aluminium International Today News
summary: This news section covers aluminum production and processing, with emphasis on industry challenges such as reducing or eliminating greenhouse gas emissions. It also includes technical articles and global industry developments relevant to sustainability and innovation.

title: ALUMINUM PROCESSING NEWS Archives
summary: This archive page includes news about aluminum processing and downstream fabrication. The featured items mention a new aluminum fabrication facility planned in Oklahoma and an Air Force contract for advanced aerospace heat-treating furnaces.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices slipped to 3,211 CNY/T on May 15, down 0.86% from the previous day. The move followed signs of a seasonal slowdown in demand and softer downstream consumption, with Mysteel warning that China’s construction steel prices may face pressure from weakening supply-demand fundamentals.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel rose to 1,134 USD/T, its highest level since January 2024. Trading Economics says the gain reflects continuing strength in the market, with HRC prices up over the past month and year.

title: EU steel prices sustained by trade measures: Assofermet
summary: Argus reports that EU mills are keeping steel prices supported mainly through trade barriers rather than stronger end-user demand. Weak manufacturing demand, geopolitical tensions, and uncertainty over new EU safeguard measures are weighing on buying interest and market sentiment.

title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
summary: SteelOrbis highlights recent market updates across regional steel and scrap markets, including pressure on some product categories and firmer overseas demand for others. The site’s pricing data shows little day-to-day movement in early-to-mid April, suggesting a relatively stable market during that period.

title: Steel Market Update: Home
summary: Steel Market Update’s front page features several recent market stories, including a U.S. Steel price increase for OCTG, logistics disruptions affecting supply chains, and optimism in buyer sentiment. It also notes expectations that sheet prices may continue rising in most regions, with Europe remaining an exception due to soft demand.

title: US Midwest Domestic Hot-Rolled Coil Steel Futures Price Today
summary: Investing.com shows U.S. Midwest hot-rolled coil steel futures trading at 1,079.00, unchanged from the previous close. The page is mainly a market data snapshot rather than a news article, but it provides a current benchmark for U.S. steel coil pricing.

title: To Meet Demand, JSW Steel Plans Steel Capacity Increase of Nearly 17M Tons
summary: JSW Steel is planning a major capacity expansion of nearly 17 million tons to keep up with strong demand. The company is allocating more than US$2.5 billion for capital projects tied to India’s infrastructure growth and rising steel consumption.

title: NMDC Steel Attains Production Record Using Danieli QSP Technology
summary: NMDC Steel reported a production record after using Danieli’s QSP technology at its thin-slab casting and rolling setup. The plant reached 100% of rated capacity, highlighting improved performance in steelmaking operations.

title: Raw steel output continues to rise in US
summary: U.S. raw steel production continued to increase, with mill capability utilization reaching 81.4%, up from 80.4% the previous week and 76.6% a year earlier. The report points to strengthening domestic steel output and higher operating rates.

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: AISI reported domestic raw steel production of 1,880,000 net tons for the week ending May 9, 2026, with capability utilization at 81.4%. The figure was higher than the same week a year earlier, indicating continued recovery in U.S. steel production.

title: NMDC Steel Attains Production Record Using Danieli QSP Technology
summary: Danieli announced that its QSP quality strip production technology helped NMDC Steel Ltd. reach 100% rated capacity on its thin-slab casting and rolling configuration. The update highlights an operational innovation in steel production technology, with a focus on improving capacity and process performance.

title: To Meet Demand, JSW Steel Plans Steel Capacity Increase of Nearly 17M Tons
summary: JSW Steel is planning a major capacity expansion backed by more than US$2.5 billion in capital projects. The move reflects the ongoing push to scale steel production to meet infrastructure-driven demand, with implications for industrial growth and modernization.

title: Report: China, India Control the Fate of Steel Decarbonization
summary: This report says the pace of global steel decarbonization will largely depend on China and India. It frames sustainability in steel as a major industry challenge, with the future path shaped by the largest producers and their investment decisions.

title: Danieli to Upgrade QSP Complex for EZZ Flat Steel
summary: Danieli has been selected to modernize EZZ Flat Steel’s QSP casting and rolling complex in Egypt. The project is another example of steel technology upgrades aimed at improving production efficiency and process capability.

title: Topalian: The World is Racing to Catch Up to U.S. Steelmakers
summary: Nucor CEO Leon Topalian was recognized for leadership in sustainable, safe, and efficient steel production. His remarks emphasized U.S. steelmaking as a global benchmark for innovation and environmental performance.

title: Nucor Sees Record Shipments — And the End of West Virginia Mill Construction
summary: Nucor reported record shipments in the first quarter of 2026 while its new West Virginia sheet mill moved into final construction phases. The story reflects both operational strength and continued investment in steelmaking capacity and modernization.

title: Copper futures drop to near $6.3 per pound as high prices curb buying in China
summary: Copper futures fell for a second straight session as elevated prices discouraged buying activity in China. The move came despite broader support from improving Chinese manufacturing activity and ongoing market sensitivity to geopolitical tensions.

title: Copper futures steadied around $5.9/lb after five straight losses on stronger China manufacturing data
summary: Copper prices stabilized after a stretch of declines, helped by stronger-than-expected expansion in China’s manufacturing sector. However, the market remained pressured by uncertainty tied to Middle East conflict risks.

title: Copper Nears Record High as Traders Tune Out Trump's Iran Rejection
summary: Copper prices moved close to record highs as traders focused more on demand and supply fundamentals than on geopolitical headlines. The article suggests market participants were largely discounting near-term Middle East risk in pricing.

title: Copper price nears record on China demand, supply worries
summary: Copper rallied near record levels amid strong demand expectations from China and concerns about supply constraints. The report frames the market as tight, with bullish fundamentals outweighing short-term volatility.

title: Copper: Prices hold near records despite Iran risks
summary: Copper prices remained elevated even as geopolitical risks from Iran continued to influence broader markets. The piece emphasizes that supply-demand dynamics were keeping copper near historic highs.

title: Copper extends retreat on faster inflation and stronger dollar
summary: Copper weakened as inflation data and a stronger U.S. dollar pressured commodity prices. The market retreat highlighted sensitivity to macroeconomic conditions alongside demand expectations.

title: Copper hits record $6.39 as Chile faces Codelco audit scandal
summary: Copper touched a record high amid concerns surrounding Chile’s Codelco and a related audit scandal. The story points to supply-side uncertainty adding to already strong copper market momentum.

title: Copper prices rise to record highs on AI demand
summary: Copper climbed to record levels as AI-related infrastructure spending boosted demand expectations. The article links the metal’s strength to data center growth and electrification needs.

title: Codelco output questioned over 20,000-tonne gap
summary: Preliminary audit findings raised fresh governance concerns at Chile's state copper miner Codelco amid questions about a 20,000-tonne output gap. The report adds to wider scrutiny of Chilean copper production just as the market is focused on supply constraints and capacity issues.

title: Copper price retreats from record close as purchases in China slow
summary: Copper futures slipped after an eight-day rally as buying in China eased, even though broader supply tightness remains a major theme. The move highlights how near-record prices are being balanced by concerns about demand momentum and production constraints.

title: Copper futures drop amid Chilean mine cuts and AI demand
summary: Copper futures fell about 1% despite ongoing worries about supply after Chile's three largest mines posted a year-over-year production decline of roughly 43,000 metric tons. The report also notes sulfuric acid shortages and rising input costs, which are adding pressure to producers.

title: Copper Rally Gains Momentum as Supply Disruptions Drive Market
summary: The discussion says copper's rally is being fueled by supply disruptions, resilient Chinese demand, and growing consumption from data center infrastructure. It also points to risks in the Middle East and Peru that could further tighten global supply.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: This article examines how emerging technologies and supportive policy could help close the widening copper supply-demand gap. It highlights innovations in primary sulfide ore processing, mining efficiency, and reducing environmental impacts such as water use and habitat destruction.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: The piece explores copper’s critical role in the clean energy transition, emphasizing its importance for efficient electricity transmission and lower energy losses. It notes that rising demand from electrification and renewables is pushing miners and technology providers toward more sustainable and efficient practices.

title: Sustainable and efficient operations for copper miners
summary: EY discusses how copper miners are adapting to strong demand by investing in operational efficiency, digitalization, and lower-emission production methods. The article also covers efforts to reduce water and energy use and expand renewable power in mining operations.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: CSIRO highlights copper as a key enabler of the energy transition and points to the industry's increasing focus on recycling, resource efficiency, and technological innovation. It also emphasizes responsible mining practices, including reduced water and energy use and stronger community engagement.

title: In the News
summary: This is a news hub collecting recent market, policy, mining, energy, and sustainability developments related to copper. It is useful as a source for tracking ongoing industry innovation and sustainability coverage.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Nickel prices slipped 2.93% to $18,586.98 per ton after a sharp run-up, with the move attributed mainly to profit-taking and a temporary pause in momentum. The article also highlights ongoing supply-side support from Indonesia's mining quota cuts, sulfur shortages, and a weaker U.S. dollar, which continue to underpin the broader market trend.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel fell to $18,580 per tonne, down 1.90% on the day, as traders took profits after a strong rally to a recent two-year high near $19,600. Despite the pullback, the market remains supported by supply disruptions, Indonesia's quota cuts, and tighter raw material availability for downstream processors.

title: Nornickel says nickel output flat year-on-year in first quarter
summary: Nornickel reported first-quarter nickel output of 42,000 metric tons, unchanged from a year earlier. The company also kept its full-year guidance unchanged, signaling stable production levels despite broader market volatility.

title: Nickel's Long-Term Bull Cycle Gains Momentum Amid ...
summary: This video discusses the nickel market’s relative price stability and the role of supply management in Indonesia and the Philippines. It also highlights project permitting progress in Canada and emerging deep-sea mining plans that could affect future nickel supply and capacity.

title: Nickel Enters a New Era as Indonesia Tightens Supply and Prices ...
summary: The video argues that nickel pricing is being reshaped by tighter Indonesian supply controls and rising production costs. It also notes inventory declines and higher sulfur costs as factors limiting output and supporting prices.

title: Nickel gains momentum amid Indonesian restrictions and declining global inventories
summary: Nickel prices rose as Indonesian quota restrictions reduced near-term ore supply and global inventories continued to fall. The article also notes operational impacts, including Eramet suspending activity at Weda Bay after exhausting its annual ore quota.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices eased after a rally to a recent high, but the broader trend remained supported by supply disruptions and tighter raw material availability. The update highlights Indonesian quota cuts and sulfur-related processing constraints as key supply-side pressures.

title: New EV battery recycling process recovers 99% pure nickel and cobalt
summary: Researchers have developed an eco-friendly recycling technology that extracts 99% pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries. The process is relevant to nickel sustainability because it could reduce reliance on primary mining while improving the circularity of battery materials.

title: Nickel Market Dynamics and the Security of the Battery Supply Chain
source: University of Texas at Austin (PDF memo)
summary: This memo outlines how nickel demand is rising rapidly, especially due to battery cathode use, and discusses implications for the battery supply chain. It also notes that clean-energy deployment could drive substantial growth in nickel demand by 2040.

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: This article focuses on innovations in mining and refining that aim to reduce the cost and environmental footprint of nickel production. It highlights how sustainability and process improvements are reshaping the nickel market.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: The Nickel Institute's blog covers sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production. It is a useful source for industry commentary on how nickel is being produced and used more efficiently.

title: Paolo Rocca steps down as Tenaris CEO amid tensions with Milei
summary: Paolo Rocca is stepping down as chief executive of Tenaris after more than two decades in the role. Gabriel Podskubka, currently COO, is set to take over as CEO while Rocca remains chairman and president of Techint Group.

title: Tenaris announced that its Board of Directors appointed Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer
summary: Tenaris confirmed the appointment of Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer. The company also stated that Paolo Rocca will continue serving as Chairman of the Board.

title: News listing | Tenaris
summary: Tenaris’s newsroom highlights recent company updates, including a leadership change, a geothermal partnership, and an acquisition agreement in Romania. The listing provides links to several current corporate announcements relevant to the company’s steel pipe business.

title: Prysmian Daily News Update: EU industrial policy, cables exclusion, and market reaction
summary: This update highlights Prysmian’s response to the EU’s proposed Industrial Acceleration Act, noting that cables were excluded from the initiative. It also frames the development in the context of broader pressure on the copper cable market and related industry policy discussions.

title: Prysmian Daily News Update Podcast Republic
summary: A recent episode references Italy-U.S. tensions, rising Prysmian targets, and growing gas risks. While not a standard news article, it reflects current company and sector developments relevant to Prysmian’s cable business.
